title: TMS-Insula Stimulation Protocol
description: Single-pulse TMS is applied over the right posterior insula to transiently disrupt interoceptive precision signals. APGI predicts that insula disruption reduces Πⁱ_eff, thereby raising the ignition threshold θₜ and reducing conscious access rates for near-threshold stimuli.

exclusion_criteria: Personal or first-degree family history of epilepsy
participants.tms_screening: Safety checklist per Rossi et al. (2021) Clin. Neurophysiol.

apparatus.TMS_system: Magstim Rapid² with 70 mm figure-of-eight coil
apparatus.EMG: BrainAmp ExG for MEP amplitude monitoring (safety check)

trial_structure.response: Visibility rating 1–4 (PAS scale), response window 2000 ms

dependent_variables: PAS rating distribution per TMS condition
Estimated θₜ shift under TMS (fitted APGI model)
Estimated Πⁱ_eff under active vs. sham TMS
primary_hypothesis: Active insula TMS reduces mean PAS rating relative to sham, consistent with a TMS-induced reduction in Πⁱ_eff and consequent elevation of θₜ.
primary_analysis: Ordinal mixed-effects model: PAS_rating ~ TMS_condition * contrast + (TMS_condition | participant)
